blocks.title: The E-Commerce Turnaround Challenge
blocks.description: One-way teaching view for structured decision-making. Students run all computations in external notebooks.

items.label: Objective
items.value: Recover margin with segmentation, campaign ROI control, and policy optimization

items.label: Delivery Model
items.value: Instructor-to-student only; no learner data captured in app

blocks.title: Scenario
blocks.body: The company is bleeding margin from inefficient marketing and high return costs. Use K-Means persona logic to justify profitable campaign and operations decisions.

blocks.title: Data Purge and Naming Ceremony
blocks.description: Segment only on meaningful financial behavior and justify cluster count clearly.
tasks: Select financially meaningful features and explicitly drop vanity/noise dimensions.
Choose k using elbow evidence, visual separation, and operational practicality.
Assign cluster persona names from centroid evidence.
hints: Prioritize spend, order frequency, return behavior, discount dependence, and recency signals.
Identifiers and demographic/browser columns are usually poor clustering distance dimensions.
A useful k yields distinct personas that your team can action, not fragmented micro-clusters.

blocks.title: Merchandising Matrix (ROI Challenge)
blocks.description: Map Campaign A/B/C to cluster personas with economically defensible logic.
tasks: Assign exactly one campaign to one cluster persona each.
Use centroid values to calculate AOV and projected profit for each assignment.
Reject assignments that fail profitability constraints.

formulas.title: AOV Formula
formulas.formula: AOV = Centroid Total Spend / Centroid Total Orders

formulas.title: Projected Profit Formula
formulas.formula: Projected Profit = (Cluster Size * Conversion Rate * AOV) - (Cluster Size * Campaign Cost)

hints: High-cost campaigns should only be assigned where expected unit economics justify spend.
Validate projected net profit numerically before finalizing any mapping.
It is normal for some clusters to receive no campaign.

blocks.title: Zero-Sum Policy Game (Operations)
blocks.description: Use penalty savings to fund premium service while controlling return-bleed impact.
tasks: Choose one cluster for Premium Policy (free overnight shipping + free returns).
Choose one toxic cluster for Penalty Policy ($12 restocking fee).
Prove the penalty target has sufficient return-bleed to justify this move.

formulas.title: Return Bleed Formula
formulas.formula: Return Bleed = Cluster Size * Centroid Total Orders * Centroid Return Rate * 12.00

hints: Premium policy should favor high-value, lower-risk personas.
Penalty policy should target highest aggregate bleed, not only highest return rate.
Defend decisions using cluster size and centroid evidence together.
blocks.summaryTemplate: We are penalizing [Cluster X] because our calculations show they are bleeding $____ from our logistics budget. We will recover those funds and use them to pay for the VIP experience of [Cluster Y].
